gaps: eval.sh is 2500 lines with no tests
beliefs YAML parser reimplements YAML with grep/sed
fallback score hardcoded 30 masks failures
RHINO_DIR used before defined in eval.sh:241
rubric generation runs in background, first eval never benefits
scoring.evidence: Parser rewrite fixed the unparseable problem. Core promise works — produces honest numbers with delta tracking. Held back by 2500-line single file with no tests.

gaps: grade.sh compares against oldest history entry not score at prediction time
pattern priority bugs in parse_claim
consolidation writes to wrong knowledge zone
trail.sh parasitic on /go session files
no tests for grade.sh despite 20 regex patterns
strengths: session_start shows grading summary with wrong predictions inline
learning.evidence: The learning loop mechanically closes. 20 patterns up from 8. Confirmation writes and staleness detection are real. Grading accuracy is fragile (wrong history bisection, consolidation zone bugs).

gaps: task generation copy-pasted across 7 skills
/rhino underdeveloped (5 files vs avg 10.75)
no dependency checking in scripts (jq, python3)
technique routing in /ideate hardcoded
0 external users — viability projected not proven
strengths: 86 files across 8 skills — thin orchestrators with real scripts
/go has most complete build loop of any CC skill
commands.evidence: The Anthropic guide rewrite landed. Skills are genuinely thin orchestrators backed by 86 real files. Scripts do real work. +16 delta is real.

gaps: manual install symlinks 3 of 19 commands
plugin mode offloads to unverified systems
no test for Linux compatibility
install.evidence: Plugin mode works. Manual path partial. New user gets guidance after install.

gaps: promote still basic
no file locking for concurrent writes
decay only flags, doesn't auto-kill
todo.evidence: CRUD works. Decay detection implemented. Functional for personal use.

gaps: no comparison table vs alternatives
walkthrough is aspirational (recipe app doesn't exist)
docs.evidence: README communicates what rhino-os is and who it's for. Positions against code quality tools.
